# Top 50 WAF Documents by SEO Value

Ranked by estimated search traffic value across 5 factors. Each factor scores 0–20 for a maximum of 100.

**Generated:** 2026-03-31
**Documents scanned:** 119

## Scoring System

| Factor | Weight | What It Measures | How It Scores |
|--------|--------|------------------|---------------|
| **Topic Demand** | 0–20 | Search volume for the document's topic | Matches document title, description, and body against 24 keyword clusters weighted by estimated monthly search volume (mega: 50K+, high: 10K–50K, medium: 1K–10K, niche: 100–1K). Title matches score 2×, description 1×, body mentions 0.5× (capped). |
| **Title & Meta** | 0–20 | Search engine optimization of title and description | Rewards specific (non-generic) titles, action-oriented verbs, high-demand keywords in title/description, ideal meta description length (100–170 chars), and SERP-friendly title length (30–65 chars). Index pages penalized. |
| **Content Authority** | 0–20 | Depth and structure that Google rewards | Word count (1,500+ = max), heading structure (5+ H2s), presence of Why section, resources section, Next steps, and code examples. Thin pages (<400 words) score near zero. |
| **Implementer Value** | 0–20 | Answers specific tool + task search queries | Multi-tool coverage, "how to" pattern density, code example count, and depth of individual tool coverage. Implementers search for working examples and specific tool combinations. |
| **Competitive Advantage** | 0–20 | HashiCorp's unique authority vs cloud vendor docs | HashiCorp tool mentions (we're THE authority), HCP-specific content, multi-tool integration stories, WAF-specific framing. Penalizes generic cloud topics where AWS/Azure/GCP docs dominate. |

**Score interpretation:**
- **80–100:** High-value page — strong search demand, well-optimized, authoritative
- **60–79:** Good potential — strong topic but may need content or optimization improvements
- **40–59:** Moderate value — either niche topic or needs significant content work
- **Below 40:** Low SEO value — thin content, generic topic, or poor optimization
